item.title: Notes on the xAI/Anthropic data center deal 摘要
item.description:
Anthropic announced a deal with SpaceX/xAI to use all capacity of the Colossus 1 data center, which has a controversial environmental record involving unpermitted gas turbines linked to increased hospital admissions. Critics, including Andy Masley, condemned the choice. xAI is retaining the larger Colossus 2 for its own work. Separately, xAI issued a two-week deprecation notice for Grok 4.1 Fast and other models, frustrating developers. Elon Musk stated he approved the lease after meeting with Anthropic and reserved the right to reclaim compute if Anthropic's AI harms humanity.
标签: Anthropic, xAI, Colossus data center, AI ethics, environmental impact, AI energy usage, Grok deprecation, Elon Musk, data center controversy, AI compute

item.title: Behind the Scenes Hardening Firefox with Claude Mythos Preview 摘要
item.description:
Mozilla used early access to Claude Mythos Preview to harden Firefox security, finding and fixing hundreds of vulnerabilities. Previously, AI-generated bug reports were mostly low-quality noise, but improved models and better harnessing techniques transformed the process. Firefox's defense-in-depth measures blocked many attempts. Mozilla fixed 20-30 security bugs per month in 2025, but in April 2026 that number jumped to 423, including a 20-year-old XSLT bug and a 15-year-old <legend> element bug.
标签: firefox, mozilla, security, ai, generative-ai, llms, anthropic, claude, ai-security-research, bug-hunting

item.title: Smoothed polygons 摘要
item.description:
This post explores a method for creating smooth approximations of polygons by multiplying linear functions that define the sides. The key idea is to define functions L_i(x,y) such that each side of the polygon corresponds to L_i=0, then form the product f(x,y)=L_1 L_2 L_3. The level sets f(x,y)=c give straight lines when c=0 and smooth rounded shapes for small c>0. The approach is general and can be applied to any polygon or curved arcs. Examples are given for a triangle, showing level sets for various c values.
标签: mathematics, geometry, polygons, smoothing, level sets, linear functions, approximation, visualization

item.title: Big Words 摘要
item.description:
Simon Willison created a simple web tool called 'Big Words' that generates text slides from URL query string parameters. The tool was built for his vibe-coded macOS presentations tool, which only accepts URLs. Users can customize text, colors, gradient, font size, and other options via URL parameters or by double-clicking the page to access a settings form. The tool is hosted at tools.simonwillison.net/big-words and supports features like gradient backgrounds, font weight, and drop shadows.
标签: vibe-coding, tools, presentation, web-tool, slide-generator, customization, URL-parameters, gradient, font-styling

item.title: llm-gemini 0.31 摘要
item.description:
llm-gemini 0.31 has been released, marking the Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite model as generally available (no longer a preview). The model was previously previewed in March 2026, and the non-preview version is unchanged. This update is part of Simon Willison's LLM tool ecosystem, enabling access to Google's Gemini models via command line.
标签: llm-gemini, release, gemini, llm, google, generative-ai, ai, llms, flash-lite, preview

item.title: Prolost Watches 1.0 摘要
item.description:
Stu Maschwitz launched Prolost Watches, an iPhone app for managing watch collections, as a one-time purchase with no subscription, ads, or server. The app logs daily watch choices, provides wear insights, maps, event resurfacing, and finance tracking. Developer John Gruber beta tested it but found he prefers not to track his watches, similar to his view on sleep tracking. Maschwitz built the app using Bitrig, a native Mac app for creating SwiftUI apps, which sits between easy tools like Lovable and terminal-based Claude Code. Apple previously cracked down on iOS vibe-coding apps, limiting iPhone-based app creation.
标签: iPhone, watch collection, app development, Bitrig, SwiftUI, vibe coding, Apple, privacy, one-time purchase, productivity
